market_context: Markets facing volatile conditions with oil surging above $105/bbl due to Iran-US tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Tesla's mixed Q1 earnings beat expectations but guidance disappointed, while geopolitical uncertainty continues to impact sentiment despite extended ceasefire.

predictions.reasoning: Oil prices surging above $105/bbl creates inflation concerns and margin pressure for broad market. Despite ceasefire extension, shipping disruptions in Strait of Hormuz continue with 4-5M barrels/day demand destruction. Rising VIX (+5.6%) signals increased volatility ahead.

Tesla's Q1 earnings beat ($0.41 vs $0.37 expected) but revenue missed and management announced $25B capex guidance vs prior $20B. Stock initially rose 4% after-hours but gave up gains after Musk's spending warnings. Negative sentiment likely to persist into regular trading.

Oil prices have surged from ~$94 to over $105/bbl in recent days on Iran-US tensions. Brent crude at $107.14 and WTI at $97.40 with supply disruption fears intact. Energy sector should benefit directly from sustained higher oil prices and geopolitical risk premium.

Rising geopolitical tensions and oil-driven inflation concerns could drive flight-to-safety flows into Treasuries. Initial jobless claims data (forecast 212K vs 207K prior) may show labor market softening. 10Y yield at 4.32% provides potential for bond rally if risk-off sentiment dominates.

Broad tech weakness expected as oil surge threatens margins and growth outlook. Tesla's guidance disappointment may weigh on high-multiple tech names. Despite AI optimism, geopolitical uncertainty and rising energy costs create headwinds for semiconductor stocks trading at premium valuations.
